title: Coordinate inferlets through engine topics
tagline: In Pie the engine hosts topics. User code publishes and subscribes through the broker, so producers and consumers hand off work without a conductor process pulling and pushing. This recording runs the three stages in one inferlet for a reproducible one-shot demo.
question: 3-stage writer's room: idea → plot → dialogue over engine topics.

naive.label: With Stock API
naive.sublabel: client-orchestrated — every hop costs a round-trip

events.value: stage 1 ▸ idea
A young girl wandered through the park, her mind drifting to the day she first met her favorite book.
→ ship to coordinator → coordinator queues → ship to plot worker
client RTT: 100 ms
stage 2 ▸ plot

Setup: A girl walks the park, lost in thought.
Confrontation: She finds a dusty book on a bench.
Resolution: The book remembers her.
→ ship to coordinator → ship to dialogue worker
client RTT: 100 ms
stage 3 ▸ dialogue
"Did you ever wonder if the book you read was reading you?"
about 600 ms  •  2 hops  •  RTT overhead about 200 ms
stage 1 ▸ idea
A young girl wandered through the park, her mind drifting to the day she first met her favorite book.
broadcast on "demo-idea-to-plot" → subscriber received  (in-engine)
stage 2 ▸ plot

Setup: A girl walks the park, lost in thought.
Confrontation: She finds a dusty book on a bench.
Resolution: The book remembers her.
broadcast on "demo-plot-to-dialogue" → subscriber received  (in-engine)
stage 3 ▸ dialogue
"Did you ever wonder if the book you read was reading you?"
about 310 ms  •  2 hops  •  0 RTT  •  about 1.9× wall-time

naive.value:
# Coordinator orchestrates each hop with HTTP RTTs.
idea=$(curl /v1/responses \
  -d '{"model":"qwen3-0.6b", "input":"Story seed: a park"}')

plot=$(curl /v1/responses \
  -d "{\"model\":\"qwen3-0.6b\", \"input\":\"Plot for: $idea\"}")

dialogue=$(curl /v1/responses \
  -d "{\"model\":\"qwen3-0.6b\", \"input\":\"Climax line for: $plot\"}")
